seoTitle: Use Monday.com for Scheduling: 2026 Setup Guide
metaDescription: Build a scheduling system in Monday.com with Calendar and Gantt views, shift columns, automated reminders, and a public booking form.
intro: Coordinating shifts, meetings, and resources across a team is hard without a shared source of truth. This guide shows how to build a scheduling board in Monday.com, from structuring bookings and shifts to spotting conflicts with Calendar and Gantt views, and even letting clients request time slots through a public form. It's built for anyone tired of managing schedules through scattered messages.

steps.heading: Create your scheduling board
steps.body: Set up a new board and organize groups such as Upcoming Bookings, Active Shifts, and Completed Sessions.

steps.heading: Add scheduling columns
steps.body: Use Date, Timeline, Week, and People columns to assign tasks, set time windows, and show shift ownership.

steps.heading: Switch to Calendar and Gantt views
steps.body: Use these views to visualize team bandwidth and catch scheduling conflicts before they happen.

steps.heading: Add a public form for requests
steps.body: Set up a Monday.com Form View so clients or team members can request meeting slots that feed directly into your master timeline.

steps.heading: Automate shift reminders
steps.body: Configure automations to notify team members when a shift is assigned or when a scheduled date arrives.

steps.heading: Monitor workload distribution
steps.body: Use the Workload widget to manage resource allocation and prevent overbooking across a distributed team.

faq.question: Can clients book time slots without a Monday.com account?
faq.answer: Yes, you can set up a public-facing Form View that lets clients or team members request slots without logging into the workspace.

faq.question: How do I catch scheduling conflicts before they happen?
faq.answer: Switch between Calendar and Gantt views, which are designed to make overlapping bookings and conflicts visible instantly.

faq.question: Can I manage shift reminders automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, low-code automations can notify team members when a shift is assigned or when its scheduled date arrives.

faq.question: Does this handle multiple time zones?
faq.answer: The board supports managing time zones and recurring calendar events as part of scheduling a distributed workforce.
